QF.id: XVI Clearing, Settlement and Risk Management_21

question.text: Quality variations in physically delivered commodities are handled through:

answer.text: Premium/discount adjustments to the delivery price based on actual vs specified quality parameters
Rejection of all deliveries that don't exactly match specifications
No adjustments — all qualities are accepted equally
A flat penalty regardless of quality variation

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Quality variations within acceptable ranges are handled through premium/discount adjustments. E.g., if specified oil content is 48% but actual is 46%, a discount is applied. If .999 gold vs .995, a premium applies. **The Trap:** Not all non-exact deliveries are rejected. Not all accepted equally. Not a flat penalty. **Cross-Exam Context:** Quality adjustment is unique to physical commodity delivery. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Understand the quality tolerance bands for your commodity — deliveries outside acceptable ranges are rejected outright, while those within range get price adjustments.
